title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Dr. Campbell questions Lucy about the dinner she prepared, which included cream of mushroom soup, curry chicken rice, cream wine dessert, and bacon chicken liver. Lucy explains she used mushrooms, chicken broth, milk, cheese flour, and lemon juice for the soup. Campbell notes that he has investigated Lucy's background and knows she has no improper connections to the Crakenespe family members Cedric, Harold, or Alfred. He collects leftover curry, mushroom soup, and spicy sauce from a clay jar for testing. Campbell expresses deep concern for Emma and Mr. Corin Soop, noting his desire to protect them from potential harm caused by Corin's sons seeking inheritance.

Campbell informs Bacon that his preliminary tests confirm the presence of arsenic in the curry. He suggests the poisoner used arsenic to mimic the appearance of mushroom poisoning to mislead investigators. Campbell notes that Miss Esperanza was the only one not poisoned, though he considers the possibility that she might have ingested a smaller dose to avoid suspicion. He advises Bacon to inform the victims they have been poisoned with arsenic to disrupt the murderer's plan.

A phone call from the nurse reveals that a situation has worsened. Campbell and Bacon learn that Alfred has died. Bacon explains to Claddock that the nurse was overwhelmed while caring for five people: Corin Soop, Emma, Alfred, Harold, and Cedric. While the nurse was attending to a noisy Corin Soop, she provided Alfred with tea containing glucose, after which he died. While Johnstone suggests the death might be a relapse, Campbell suspects continued arsenic poisoning. Claddock observes that Alfred's death provides no benefit to the heirs, whereas the old man's death would.
